title: rubus rosanthus
intro: Rubus rosanthus, also known as the rose-leaved raspberry or Italian bramble, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The leaves and roots of the plant have been used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been reported to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Additionally, the plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including sore throats, coughs, and colds.
